div.p: born to die, and all men must rise;1 all must enter eternity.2

In order for you to receive your children to yourselves you must have a promise—some ordinance; some blessing,3 in order to ascend above principalities,4 or else it may be an angel.5 They must rise just as they died;6 we can there hail our lovely infants with the same glory—the same loveliness in the celestial glory,7 where they all enjoy alike.8 They differ in stature, in size, the same glorious spirit gives them the likeness of glory and bloom; the old man with his silvery hairs will glory in bloom and beauty. No man can describe it to you—no man can write it.9

When did I ever teach anything wrong from this stand? When was I ever confounded? I want to triumph in Israel10 before I depart hence and am no more seen.11 I never told you I was perfect; but there is no error in the revelations which I have taught. Must I, then, be thrown away as a thing of naught?12

I enjoin for your consideration—add to your faith, virtue, love, &c. I say, in the name of the Lord, if these things are in you, you shall be fruitful.13 I testify that no man has power to reveal it but myself14—things in heaven, in earth,15 and hell; and all shut your mouths for the future. I commend you all to God,16 that you may inherit all things;17 and may God add His blessing. Amen. (May 12, 1844.) DHC 6:363–367.
div.div: The Prophet’s Dreams on Conditions of Apostates at Nauvoo
div.p: In the evening I attended meeting in the Seventies’ Hall. George J. Adams preached and I made some observations afterwards, and related a dream which I had a short time since. I thought I was riding out in my carriage, and my guardian angel was along with me. We went past the Temple and had not gone much further before we espied two large snakes so fast locked together that neither of them had any power. I inquired18 of my guide what I was to understand by that. He answered, “Those snakes represent Dr. Foster and Chauncey L. Higbee. They are your enemies and desire to destroy you;19 but you see they are so fast locked together that they have no power of themselves to hurt you.” I then thought I was riding up Mulholland street, but my guardian angel was not along with me. On arriving at the prairie, I was overtaken
